one_line: The shadowed Mauryan emperor between Chandragupta and Ashoka, a consolidator-king linked to both Indian and Hellenistic worlds.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s to early 40s, tall and solidly built with a ruler’s bearing and a warrior’s shoulders.
appearance.face: Oval face with strong jaw, medium-deep brown complexion, straight prominent nose, thick arched brows, short neatly trimmed moustache, faint stubble on chin, a small circular birthmark (bindu) at his right temple, default expression calm and observant with a restrained, knowing half-smile.
appearance.hair: Black hair drawn into a high topknot (ushnisha-style knot) at the back of the crown, sides and back smoothly pulled up, front hairline clean; a narrow gold fillet-band around the head above the brow with a small central red stone, no helmet or turban.
appearance.costume: A mid-thigh length white cotton angarakha-style wrap tunic crossing left over right, tied with inner ties, worn over a deep indigo-blue cotton dhoti wrapped tightly and pleated in front and reaching mid-calf, bare feet in simple brown leather sandals; over the left shoulder and across the chest a saffron-ochre fine cotton uttariya (shawl) with a subtle narrow maroon border, draped so it can be pulled up like a veil in court; jewellery limited to a single broad gold kara (bangle) on the right wrist, a plain gold torque-like neck collar close to the skin, and small simple gold ear-studs; at his waist a narrow dark-red cloth belt holds a modest plain brown leather pouch, the overall look clean, regal and restrained, evoking Mauryan court dress with minimal ornament.
appearance.signature: An iron-tipped, dark-wood royal staff of authority about his own height, straight with a slightly thicker base, wrapped near the grip with a narrow spiral of indigo-dyed leather and capped at the top with a small, simple lotus-bud finial in dull gold, carried in his right hand or resting beside him.

ref_desc: Bindusara Amitraghata is a tall, solidly built late-30s Mauryan king with an oval medium-brown face, small bindu birthmark at his right temple, black hair in a high topknot with a gold headband, wearing a white angarakha over deep indigo dhoti, saffron-ochre uttariya, minimal gold jewellery, brown sandals, and carrying a lotus-tipped dark-wood iron-staff, in a white, deep indigo, saffron-ochre, maroon, and dull-gold palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Bindusara Amitraghata, a late-30s Mauryan emperor: tall and solidly built, with an oval medium-brown face, strong jaw, straight nose, thick brows, a short neat moustache, faint chin stubble, and a tiny circular birthmark at his right temple; his black hair is pulled tightly into a high topknot at the back of the crown, encircled by a narrow gold fillet-band with a small central red stone, no turban or helmet. Show him in his fixed costume: a mid-thigh white cotton wrap angarakha crossing left over right, over a deep indigo-blue dhoti wrapped and pleated to mid-calf, with simple brown leather sandals; a saffron-ochre cotton uttariya with a subtle maroon border is slung from his left shoulder across his chest and around his back. Add restrained jewellery only: a plain gold torque close around his neck, one broad gold bangle on his right wrist, and small gold ear-studs. At his waist is a narrow dark-red cloth belt with a small plain brown leather pouch. His signature prop is a straight dark-wood staff as tall as he is, with an iron tip at the base, a narrow spiral of indigo leather wrapping near the grip, and a small dull-gold lotus-bud finial at the top; show him holding or resting this staff prominently. Use a palette dominated by white, deep indigo blue, saffron ochre, maroon, and dull gold, and keep his default expression calm and observant with a subtle, knowing half-smile.
